seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents in OpenAI Assistants API Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix context window management for ai agents in OpenAI Assistants API agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In OpenAI Assistants API, Context Window Management issues arise when agent threads accumulate excessive messages, files, and tool outputs, silently exceeding model token limits during runs. This leads to truncated context, hallucinations in function calls, and degraded performance as the API doesn't enforce hard limits on thread history. Agents built with persistent threads for multi-turn conversations are particularly vulnerable, manifesting as incomplete reasoning or irrelevant responses.

howItManifests.scenario: Long-running customer support agent with persistent thread
howItManifests.symptom: Run completes but final response references early conversation details incorrectly
howItManifests.impact: Agent loses conversational coherence, frustrating users and requiring manual thread resets

howItManifests.scenario: Code interpreter agent processing iterative debugging sessions
howItManifests.symptom: Function calls fail validation with hallucinated arguments after 50+ turns
howItManifests.impact: Development workflow breaks as agent can't maintain execution context

howItManifests.scenario: File search agent with growing attachment list
howItManifests.symptom: Retrieval tool returns empty results despite relevant files
howItManifests.impact: RAG pipeline fails silently, delivering hallucinated answers

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-tool agent chaining function calls over time
howItManifests.symptom: Thread token count exceeds 128k silently, causing 400 errors on new runs
howItManifests.impact: Production agents crash unpredictably during peak usage

detection.name: Thread Token Limit Monitor
detection.description: Calculates total token usage across thread messages and detects when approaching model limits

detection.name: Run Completion Token Overflow Check
detection.description: Inspects completed run metadata for truncation indicators and excessive input tokens
detection.code:
import openai
from typing import Dict, Any

def detect_context_overflow(run: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
    # Check for truncation in usage
    if hasattr(run, 'usage') and run.usage:
        if run.usage.prompt_tokens + run.usage.completion_tokens > 127000:
            return True
    
    # Check for incomplete tool calls (common overflow symptom)
    if run.status == 'completed' and run.required_action:
        return True
    
    # Check last message for context loss indicators
    messages = openai.beta.threads.messages.list(run.thread_id)
    last_msg = messages.data[0]
    
    truncation_indicators = [
        'i forgot',
        'earlier context',
        'previous conversation',
        "don't remember",
        'context window'
    ]
    
    if last_msg.role == 'assistant':
        content = last_msg.content[0].text.value.lower()
        if any(indicator in content for indicator in truncation_indicators):
            return True
    
    return False

detection.name: Message History Analyzer
detection.description: TypeScript function to analyze thread message patterns for context bloat

fixes.name: Smart Thread Pruning
fixes.description: Automatically removes old messages while preserving critical context
fixes.code:
from openai import OpenAI
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def prune_thread_context(thread_id: str, max_messages: int = 50, days_old: int = 7):
    client = OpenAI()
    messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id=thread_id, limit=200)
    
    cutoff_date = datetime.now() - timedelta(days=days_old)
    keep_messages = []
    delete_ids = []
    
    # Prioritize: system messages, recent convos, tool outputs
    for msg in messages.data:
        msg_date = datetime.fromtimestamp(msg.created_at)
        is_critical = (
            msg.role == 'system' or
            msg.tool_outputs or
            msg_date > cutoff_date or
            len(keep_messages) < 10  # Keep first 10 regardless
        )
        
        if is_critical and len(keep_messages) < max_messages:
            keep_messages.append(msg)
        else:
            delete_ids.append(msg.id)
    
    # Delete old messages
    for msg_id in delete_ids:
        client.beta.threads.messages.delete(thread_id=thread_id, message_id=msg_id)
    
    # Add summary of pruned content
    if delete_ids:
        summary = f"Pruned {len(delete_ids)} old messages. Active context: {len(keep_messages)} messages."
        client.beta.threads.messages.create(
            thread_id=thread_id,
            role="system",
            content=summary
        )
    
    return len(keep_messages)

fixes.name: Token-Aware Message Batching
fixes.description: Creates summary threads for long conversations instead of letting context explode
fixes.code:
from openai import OpenAI
import tiktoken

def create_summary_thread(original_thread_id: str):
    client = OpenAI()
    encoding = tiktoken.encoding_for_model('gpt-4o')
    
    # Get recent conversation
    messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(
        thread_id=original_thread_id,
        limit=30,
        order="desc"
    )
    
    conv_summary = "Summarize this conversation concisely, preserving key facts, user preferences, and unresolved issues: "
    for msg in messages.data[:20]:  # Last 20 messages
        role = "User" if msg.role == "user" else "Assistant"
        content = msg.content[0].text.value[:1000] if msg.content else ""
        conv_summary += f"\n{role}: {content}"
    
    # Create summary thread
    new_thread = client.beta.threads.create()
    
    summary_response = client.beta.threads.messages.create(
        thread_id=new_thread.id,
        role="user",
        content=conv_summary
    )
    
    run = client.beta.threads.runs.create(
        thread_id=new_thread.id,
        assistant_id="your-summary-assistant-id"
    )
    
    return new_thread.id

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Context Window Management for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly with OpenAI Assistants API run completion webhooks, automatically detecting context window overflow by analyzing thread message counts, token estimates, and response quality metrics. When thresholds are exceeded, Vex triggers intelligent remediation workflows including thread pruning, summary thread creation, and model switching without interrupting agent operation.

The platform maintains conversation state across interventions by generating context-preserving summaries and injecting them into new threads. Vex's verification engine continuously monitors post-fix performance, ensuring remediation actually improves agent reliability rather than just masking symptoms.

Integration requires a single webhook configuration in your Assistant's run completion events, with Vex handling all detection, alerting, and automated recovery logic.
